topic: AI Coding Tools Pricing Comparison 2026: Free vs Paid Plans Broken Down

competitors.title: AI Coding Tools Pricing Comparison 2026: Free vs Paid Plans Compared
key_points: GitHub Copilot Pro is best value at $10/month with 300 premium requests, coding agent, code review, and multi-model support including Claude Opus 4.6
The $20/month tier is the new standard: Cursor Pro, Windsurf Pro, Claude Code Pro, Augment Indie, and v0 Premium all converge at $20/month
Heavy usage costs $60-200/month: Cursor Pro+ ($60), Claude Code Max 5x ($100), Cursor Ultra/Windsurf Max ($200)
Free tiers are genuinely usable in 2026: Bolt.new (1M tokens/month), GitHub Copilot Free (2,000 completions), Codex CLI (open source)
Pricing models are fragmenting: Credits, tokens, quotas, premium requests, and daily caps all coexist
Copilot overage charges $0.04 per premium request beyond plan limit — 500 overage requests adds $20
Codex CLI is ~4x more token-efficient than Claude Code per OpenAI claims
Annual billing discounts: Cursor Pro drops $20→$16/month, Copilot Pro drops $10→$8.33/month
Optimal stack for most developers: Copilot Pro ($10) + Cursor Pro/Claude Code Pro ($20) = $30/month
Cursor shifted from request-based to credit-based pricing mid-2025; Auto mode is unlimited on Pro+
Windsurf overhauled pricing March 19, 2026 from credits to daily/weekly quotas — controversial change

competitors.title: AI Coding Tools Pricing Guide — 2026 Comparison
key_points: 9 tools compared, $20/mo new standard, $200/mo power tier, 3 generous free tiers tracked
Cursor and Windsurf now charge identical prices ($20/$200/$40)
11 pricing changes tracked for AI coding tools — market in continuous flux
Gemini Code Assist offers the most generous free tier: 6,000 completions/day (~180K/month), 90x more than Copilot free tier
Augment Code removed free tier in April 2026; lowest plan is now Indie at $20/month with 40K credits
Cursor added Hobby tier at $10/mo and Pro+ at $60/mo to fill gaps between Free and Ultra
GitHub Copilot Student plan: premium models now available only via Auto mode, no manual model selection
Credits and quotas replacing flat subscriptions: Cursor (June 2025), Augment Code (October 2025), Windsurf (March 2026)
Claude Code April 2026: third-party tools billed separately, peak-hour throttling weekdays 5-11 AM PT
Cline and Aider remain fully free/open-source with BYO API key model — zero vendor lock-in

competitors.title: AI Coding Tools Pricing Comparison (2026)
key_points: Compares 9 tools: Claude Code, Cursor, GitHub Copilot, Windsurf, OpenAI Codex, Aider, Continue.dev, Zed, Replit Agent
Best free options: Aider and Continue.dev are fully open source with no tool-level cost, use BYOK or local models
Copilot at $10/mo is cheapest paid option with unlimited completions on Pro tier
Cursor Pro at $20/mo: Composer mode for multi-file edits, 500 fast requests/month on Pro, unlimited slow requests
Claude Code Max at $100-200/mo for most capable autonomous coding agent with extended usage limits
Windsurf free tier is generous with AI credits; Pro at $15/mo includes increased AI credits and priority models
Open-source tools like Aider have no tool-level limits — users pay for LLM API usage directly
Replit Agent at $25/mo (Core) for no-code prototyping and instant deployment

competitors.title: AI Code Assistant Market Size 2026: $6B | CAGR 22%
key_points: AI code assistant market estimated at $6.0 billion in 2026, growing at 22% CAGR to $43.8B by 2036
GitHub Copilot has 1.3 million paid subscribers; Cursor generates over $500M ARR
Gartner forecasts 75% of enterprise software engineers will use AI code assistants by 2028
AI contributions: approximately 42% of committed code, but many developers don't thoroughly verify AI suggestions
~9-11 million paid seats globally, blended average ~$500/year per seat
IDE extensions represent ~65% of market revenue (2026), AI-native environments ~25%, self-hosted ~10%
AI-native environments projected to grow from 25% to 45% market share by 2036
Market competitiveness score 85/100 — Microsoft, Google, AWS, JetBrains, Cursor all competing intensely
North America generates 45% of revenue; Asia expected to grow from 22% to 38% by 2036
Market maturity score only 45/100 — still in early growth phase with evolving pricing models

angles: The $20/month convergence: Why every major AI coding tool landed on the same price point — and what this means for competition and differentiation
Hidden costs and pricing traps: Overage charges, credit ambiguity, quota resets, and API unpredictability that make the headline price misleading
Free tier evaluation: Which free tiers are genuinely usable for daily coding vs. which run out in hours (Gemini Code Assist vs. Copilot vs. Cursor)
The credit-to-quota migration: Cursor (June 2025), Augment Code (Oct 2025), Windsurf (March 2026) — why vendors are abandoning flat subscriptions for consumption models
Open-source BYOK alternatives: Cline, Aider, Continue.dev offer zero-cost AI coding with your own API key — when this beats paying $20+/month
Budget-optimized stacks: Combining Copilot Free + Codex CLI ($0), Copilot Pro + Cursor Pro ($30/month), or Claude Code API for cost-conscious teams
Enterprise pricing realities: Teams pricing convergence at $19-40/seat, IP indemnity differences, and the on-premises option (Tabnine at $39-59/user/month)
API cost unpredictability: A heavy Opus 4.6 session can cost $15+ in one sitting — how to set spending limits and monitor usage

outline: Introduction: Why AI Coding Tool Pricing Got So Confusing in 2026
The $20/Month Standard: How Every Major Tool Converged on One Price
Pricing Comparison Table: Free, Pro, Power, and Enterprise Tiers Head-to-Head
AI IDEs: Cursor vs Windsurf — Credits vs Quotas at the Same Price
Code Completion Extensions: GitHub Copilot, Tabnine, Amazon Q, Gemini Code Assist
Terminal AI Agents: Claude Code vs OpenAI Codex CLI — Token Windows vs Messages
AI App Builders: v0, Lovable, Bolt.new — Tokens and Credits
Newcomers: Augment Code and the Credit-Based Model
Open-Source BYOK Alternatives: Aider, Cline, Continue.dev — Free Forever?
What You Actually Get for Free: Free Tier Deep Dive
Hidden Costs: Overage Charges, Credit Ambiguity, and API Unpredictability
The Credit-to-Quota Migration: Why Pricing Models Keep Changing
Market Size and Growth: $6B in 2026, $43.8B by 2036
Budget-Optimized Stacks: Best Combinations at Every Price Point ($0, $10, $30, $100+)
Enterprise Considerations: Teams Pricing, IP Indemnity, and Self-Hosted Options
Conclusion: How to Pick the Right Tool Without Overpaying
